steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which statement is best supported by the passage. The key claim is that Azara's night monkeys forage more with higher lunar intensity because of their greater reliance on vision — meaning any cost of extra light is outweighed by the visual benefit.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — If the monkeysʼ foraging behavior under increased lunar intensity actually reflects a cost-benefit trade-off, their behavior should be more similar to that of European badgers than it is.: the passage explicitly explains the discrepancy, so similarity is not predicted by OFT here. ✗
  • Choice B — If the advantages that the monkeys gain from increased lunar intensity explain the change in their foraging behavior, those advantages are likely shared by some other species that are not heavily reliant on vision.: the passage ties the advantage specifically to vision reliance, ruling this out. ✗
  • Choice C — If increased lunar intensity imposed the same costs on the monkeys that it imposes on European badgers, there would be no association between lunar intensity and the monkeysʼ foraging.: the passage does not establish what would happen under that hypothetical; a positive effect could still exist. ✗
  • Choice D — If increased lunar intensity creates any disadvantages for the monkeys, those disadvantages are more than compensated for by the advantages that the monkeys gain.: the monkeys forage more with more light, so net benefits must exceed any costs — directly supported by the positive association and OFT logic. ✓
That matches Choice D — If increased lunar intensity creates any disadvantages for the monkeys, those disadvantages are more than compensated for by the advantages that the monkeys gain..
